### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v2.38.0 -m "<description of version>" 02671f612ee581eb729645dd661b4039e93cadbd
git push origin v2.38.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v9.55.0 -m "<description of version>" 36bd3fd96196c8402a87d59f1f85aa9e7c7fa7aa
git push origin v9.55.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v2.68.1 -m "<description of version>" e1e281b6aff13a6f65e4cd02dc747d10799ca75e
git push origin v2.68.1
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v5.50.0 -m "<description of version>" db02d7c03e27e70b8835f1f10e571cad0f1bd0ea
git push origin v5.50.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a NonlinearSolveFirstOrder-v1.2.0 -m "<description of version>" 3bb2eb3193e8febf170a6d975fe4657f64976887
git push origin NonlinearSolveFirstOrder-v1.2.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a SimpleNonlinearSolve-v2.1.0 -m "<description of version>" 469307d85d9fd505d8f6b9a14251c1b802acfc08
git push origin SimpleNonlinearSolve-v2.1.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v2.70.0 -m "<description of version>" 966351b8960e2d5494226cd30a79a26efa73e9b9
git push origin v2.70.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v3.27.3 -m "<description of version>" 64f696414ec602ee151b4fa66e438b894ada3581
git push origin v3.27.3
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v3.13.4 -m "<description of version>" cb9c1d695103b75a7e5e946f583d35aad4dcf6b2
git push origin v3.13.4
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v7.71.0 -m "<description of version>" 5d432999c40fe829493b396417f10567d3a3ac05
git push origin v7.71.0
```

pull request created: [JuliaRegistries/General/119227](https://github.com/JuliaRegistries/General/pull/119227)

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v2.60.0 -m "<description of version>" ca779545d57d2170bae8382cb523cfc49e11b314
git push origin v2.60.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v6.159.0 -m "<description of version>" b52cf18c2ea8d44bbdd0d2384c4a570a6277c114
git push origin v6.159.0
```

release that TagBot creates. i.e.

@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

- blah

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v2.37.0 -m "<description of version>" 951a2bffef5ecea3ee3038afab8102a8bebac657
git push origin v2.37.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a BoundaryValueDiffEqMIRKN-v1.0.0 -m "<description of version>" a4caf86639be2e6d10eb23273e209e047d8087ce
git push origin BoundaryValueDiffEqMIRKN-v1.0.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a BoundaryValueDiffEqShooting-v1.2.0 -m "<description of version>" 8c7afbde0bc0a46d6325adfd6feceb260cd886bb
git push origin BoundaryValueDiffEqShooting-v1.2.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v7.71.2 -m "<description of version>" 849d093c928c2c33d584ede9dafd20d77471e102
git push origin v7.71.2
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v2.65.0 -m "<description of version>" f939a5f605361f5fbf7e76d1c54157cffcd8fadc
git push origin v2.65.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v0.15.1 -m "<description of version>" 7b27d04defe737481e832121c4f4ac07c7c1c8f0
git push origin v0.15.1
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v2.15.0 -m "<description of version>" 37b5157a02a2e468e943a66409d8457551e69c45
git push origin v2.15.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v6.4.4 -m "<description of version>" 22850704af87aad532548c7a6b7d319b1d33a8e3
git push origin v6.4.4
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v0.1.4 -m "<description of version>" b8573fa935587ea5b45d32c12543a1c54bde4ba5
git push origin v0.1.4
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v6.157.0 -m "<description of version>" eb9f6a4f1d09553a67f5ee0d58f3056bd80448af
git push origin v6.157.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v9.45.0 -m "<description of version>" 816fde74ed572cbb0201e67f42abd32afc4594d0
git push origin v9.45.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v6.5.0 -m "<description of version>" b02fcdaf89a116d4754d8681ae45da636838d525
git push origin v6.5.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a BoundaryValueDiffEqMIRK-v1.0.1 -m "<description of version>" 41fd09c2d8cd5aa0c5c13bae211ce0b78fa3ccd2
git push origin BoundaryValueDiffEqMIRK-v1.0.1
```
Also, note the warning: Version 1.0.1 skips over 1.0.0
This can be safely ignored. However, if you want to fix this you can do so. Call register() again after making the fix. This will update the Pull request.

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v0.3.33 -m "<description of version>" 7b3751155df495f9b8d8abe5e4e6313c7667de21
git push origin v0.3.33
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v6.5.2 -m "<description of version>" 4e915a15f5513498e391279dde091e1768652a84
git push origin v6.5.2
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v2.56.2 -m "<description of version>" 208957dd59628fe78cef3a804096ce25dac3758c
git push origin v2.56.2
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v6.158.3 -m "<description of version>" 4a70fe0c3d63f40cb4e6ac5a21baa64642acaf58
git push origin v6.158.3
```
Also, note the warning: Version 6.158.3 skips over 6.158.2
This can be safely ignored. However, if you want to fix this you can do so. Call register() again after making the fix. This will update the Pull request.

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v6.70.0 -m "<description of version>" 2930ba8c7bc113aa57c5b8c41c3dcc6dc2c17dd9
git push origin v6.70.0
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v6.158.3 -m "<description of version>" df8ad4e252633418491ad8f43950692fbbe2772d
git push origin v6.158.3
```

### Tip: Release Notes

Did you know you can add release notes too? Just add markdown formatted text underneath the comment after the text
"Release notes:" and it will be added to the registry PR, and if TagBot is installed it will also be added to the
release that TagBot creates. i.e.

```
@JuliaRegistrator register

Release notes:

## Breaking changes

- blah
```

To add them here just re-invoke and the PR will be updated.


### Tagging

After the above pull request is merged, it is recommended that a tag is created on this repository for the registered package version.

This will be done automatically if the [Julia TagBot GitHub Action](https://github.com/marketplace/actions/julia-tagbot) is installed, or can be done manually through the github interface, or via:
```
git tag -a v9.47.0 -m "<description of version>" 8f2a9e4ba76f55939b71b93ca293740cc523e2cc
git push origin v9.47.0
```
